prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. A referendum was held on 6 February 2023 in the Wolayita, Gamo, Gofa, South Omo, Gedeo, and Konso Zones, as well as the Dirashe, Amaro, Burji, Ale, and Basketo special woredas of the Southern Nations, Nationalities, and Peoples' Region (SNNP) of Ethiopia. The area was asked to leave SNNP and form its own Region. This referendum followed two referendums in 2019 and 2021 in other areas. Wolayita Zone required to rehold voting
B. On 6 February 2023 a referendum was held in the Wolayita, Gamo, Gofa, South Omo, Gedeo, and Konso Zones, as well as the Dirashe, Amaro, Burji, Ale, and Basketo special woredas of the Southern Nations, Nationalities, and Peoples' Region (SNNP) of Ethiopia, on whether the included areas should leave SNNP and form their own Region. This referendum follows two previous referendums from 2019 and 2021 in other areas of the then-SNNP, both of which resulted in votes to split off into new regions. The referendum was tentatively approved, although Wolayita Zone must rehold voting after it was found that irregularities were present. The other zones and woredas involved passed resolutions to split off in July 2023, and this was conveyed to the national House of Federation by the
C. A plebiscite took place on February 6, 2023, across several administrative divisions within Ethiopia's Southern Nations, Nationalities, and Peoples' Region (SNNP). These divisions included the Wolayita, Gamo, Gofa, South Omo, Gedeo, and Konso Zones, alongside the special woredas of Dirashe, Amaro, Burji, Ale, and Basketo. The purpose of this electoral process was to determine if these specified areas desired to secede from the SNNP Region and establish an independent administrative Region of their own. This electoral event is the third of its kind, building upon two prior referendums conducted in 2019 and 2021 in different parts of what was then the SNNP. In both of those earlier instances, the outcome was a decision to separate and establish new regions. While the recent referendum received preliminary approval, a caveat emerged regarding the Wolayita Zone, which is mandated to conduct its voting anew due to the identification of procedural anomalies. Conversely, the remaining zones and woredas that participated successfully passed their respective motions to secede in July 2023, a decision subsequently communicated to the national House of Federation.

Answer:

options.A: A referendum was held on 6 February 2023 in the Wolayita, Gamo, Gofa, South Omo, Gedeo, and Konso Zones, as well as the Dirashe, Amaro, Burji, Ale, and Basketo special woredas of the Southern Nations, Nationalities, and Peoples' Region (SNNP) of Ethiopia. The area was asked to leave SNNP and form its own Region. This referendum followed two referendums in 2019 and 2021 in other areas. Wolayita Zone required to rehold voting
options.B: On 6 February 2023 a referendum was held in the Wolayita, Gamo, Gofa, South Omo, Gedeo, and Konso Zones, as well as the Dirashe, Amaro, Burji, Ale, and Basketo special woredas of the Southern Nations, Nationalities, and Peoples' Region (SNNP) of Ethiopia, on whether the included areas should leave SNNP and form their own Region. This referendum follows two previous referendums from 2019 and 2021 in other areas of the then-SNNP, both of which resulted in votes to split off into new regions. The referendum was tentatively approved, although Wolayita Zone must rehold voting after it was found that irregularities were present. The other zones and woredas involved passed resolutions to split off in July 2023, and this was conveyed to the national House of Federation by the
options.C: A plebiscite took place on February 6, 2023, across several administrative divisions within Ethiopia's Southern Nations, Nationalities, and Peoples' Region (SNNP). These divisions included the Wolayita, Gamo, Gofa, South Omo, Gedeo, and Konso Zones, alongside the special woredas of Dirashe, Amaro, Burji, Ale, and Basketo. The purpose of this electoral process was to determine if these specified areas desired to secede from the SNNP Region and establish an independent administrative Region of their own. This electoral event is the third of its kind, building upon two prior referendums conducted in 2019 and 2021 in different parts of what was then the SNNP. In both of those earlier instances, the outcome was a decision to separate and establish new regions. While the recent referendum received preliminary approval, a caveat emerged regarding the Wolayita Zone, which is mandated to conduct its voting anew due to the identification of procedural anomalies. Conversely, the remaining zones and woredas that participated successfully passed their respective motions to secede in July 2023, a decision subsequently communicated to the national House of Federation.
